pages.title: Tawuia
pages.text: $(l)Temporal Range:$() Paleoproterozoic (Statherian) - Cambrian$(br2)$(l)Habitat:$() Marine$(br2)>$(o)incertae sedis$(br2)$(l)Propagation:$() Craft with a bucket of water.$(br)$(l)Method of Obtaining:$() Shears

Tawuia hails from the late Paleoproterozoic right into the lowermost Cambrian, with fossils being found in formations across the globe. They are small, sausage-shaped forms, growing up to 2.5 cm in height. They are most commonly found as organic compressions on a rock surface, and its true affinity has been difficult to place since its discovery. Some researchers suggest it to be simple microbial structures, like Stromatolites, whilst others suggest them to be

similar to modern slime molds. These two ideas were upheld for many decades by their respective parties, but recent evidence shows that Tawuia may instead be a macroalgae, and one that may also be coenocytic, which is where a single celled organism contains multiple nuclei, and multiple nuclei within in a single cell of a multicellular organism.
